---
title: "RI HP Cross-Subsidy Diagnostics (Analysis Outputs)"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
toc: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

This notebook analyzes RI CAIRO run outputs from `s3://data.sb/switchbox/cairo/ri_hp_rates/analysis_outputs/`, comparing two specified analysis-output runs and then doing detailed diagnostics on one selected run.

```{python}
from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

import polars as pl
from plotnine import (
    aes,
    coord_flip,
    facet_grid,
    facet_wrap,
    geom_area,
    geom_col,
    geom_line,
    geom_ribbon,
    ggplot,
    labs,
    position_dodge,
    scale_fill_manual,
    theme,
    theme_minimal,
)
import sys

for _root in (Path.cwd(), *Path.cwd().parents):
    if (_root / "lib" / "rdp.py").is_file():
        sys.path.insert(0, str(_root))
        break
else:
    raise FileNotFoundError("Could not locate lib/rdp.py from current working directory.")

from lib.rdp import (
    build_bldg_id_to_load_filepath,
    build_cross_components,
    build_hourly_group_loads,
    build_hourly_heating_type_loads,
    build_tariff_components,
    find_latest_run_dir,
    load_cambium_from_parquet_s3,
    load_dist_mc_from_run,
    read_s3_csv,
    read_s3_json,
    resolve_resstock_hourly_loads_dir,
    summarize_cross_subsidy,
    summarize_cross_subsidy_by_heating_type,
    summarize_positive_distribution_hours,
)

try:
    from cairo.rates_tool.loads import _return_load
except ModuleNotFoundError as exc:
    raise ModuleNotFoundError(
        "CAIRO is not installed in this reports2 environment. "
        "Run `uv sync` in reports2 after adding cairo to dependencies."
    ) from exc


pl.Config.set_tbl_cols(-1)

RUN_BASE = "s3://data.sb/switchbox/cairo/ri_hp_rates/analysis_outputs"

# Both runs use the same Cambium MidCase P133 data; delivery_only_rev_req_passed in
# run_scenario.py controls whether supply costs are included in the revenue requirement.
_CAMBIUM_S3 = "s3://data.sb/nrel/cambium/2024/scenario=MidCase/t=2025/gea=ISONE/r=p133/data.parquet"

RUN_CONFIG = {
    "precalc_delivery": {
        "run_name": "ri_rie_run_01_precalc_delivery_up00_y2025",
        "cambium_costs": _CAMBIUM_S3,
        "target_year": 2025,
        "precalc_tariff_key": "rie_a16",
        "label_display": "Delivery (full rev-req)",
    },
    "precalc_delivery_supply": {
        "run_name": "ri_rie_run_02_precalc_delivery_supply_up00_y2025",
        "cambium_costs": _CAMBIUM_S3,
        "target_year": 2025,
        "precalc_tariff_key": "rie_a16_supply_adj",
        "label_display": "Delivery+supply (delivery-only rev-req)",
    },
}
ACTIVE_RUN_LABEL = "precalc_delivery"

RESSTOCK_LOADS = resolve_resstock_hourly_loads_dir(
    release="res_2024_amy2018_2",
    state="RI",
    upgrade="00",
)

# Resolve the most recent output directory for each run (directories are named
# {YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS}_{run_name}; find_latest_run_dir picks the lexicographically latest).
run_dirs = {
    label: find_latest_run_dir(RUN_BASE, cfg["run_name"])
    for label, cfg in RUN_CONFIG.items()
}

if ACTIVE_RUN_LABEL not in run_dirs:
    raise ValueError(
        f"ACTIVE_RUN_LABEL={ACTIVE_RUN_LABEL!r} is not in configured run labels: {list(run_dirs)}"
    )

run_dir = run_dirs[ACTIVE_RUN_LABEL]

print("Configured runs:")
for run_label, run_path in run_dirs.items():
    print(f"  - {run_label} [{RUN_CONFIG[run_label]['label_display']}]: {run_path}")
print(f"Using detailed diagnostics run: {ACTIVE_RUN_LABEL} -> {run_dir}")
```

## Load Cross-Subsidy Outputs

```{python}
run_payload = {}

for run_label, run_path in run_dirs.items():
    run_metadata = read_s3_csv(f"{run_path}/customer_metadata.csv")
    run_cross = read_s3_csv(f"{run_path}/cross_subsidization/cross_subsidization_BAT_values.csv")
    run_cross_summary = summarize_cross_subsidy(cross=run_cross, metadata=run_metadata)
    run_cross_summary = run_cross_summary.with_columns(pl.lit(run_label).alias("run_label"))

    run_cross_components = build_cross_components(run_cross_summary)
    run_cross_components = run_cross_components.with_columns(pl.lit(run_label).alias("run_label"))

    run_ht_summary = summarize_cross_subsidy_by_heating_type(cross=run_cross, metadata=run_metadata)
    run_ht_summary = run_ht_summary.with_columns(pl.lit(run_label).alias("run_label"))

    run_payload[run_label] = {
        "metadata": run_metadata,
        "cross": run_cross,
        "cross_summary": run_cross_summary,
        "cross_components": run_cross_components,
        "ht_summary": run_ht_summary,
    }

cross_summary_all = pl.concat(
    [payload["cross_summary"] for payload in run_payload.values()],
    how="vertical",
)
cross_components_all = pl.concat(
    [payload["cross_components"] for payload in run_payload.values()],
    how="vertical",
)
ht_summary_all = pl.concat(
    [payload["ht_summary"] for payload in run_payload.values()],
    how="vertical",
)

metadata = run_payload[ACTIVE_RUN_LABEL]["metadata"]
cross = run_payload[ACTIVE_RUN_LABEL]["cross"]
cross_summary = run_payload[ACTIVE_RUN_LABEL]["cross_summary"]
cross_components = run_payload[ACTIVE_RUN_LABEL]["cross_components"]
ht_summary = run_payload[ACTIVE_RUN_LABEL]["ht_summary"]

cross_summary_all.sort(["run_label", "group"])
```

```{python}
# Customer counts by group (weighted)
cross_summary_all.select("run_label", "group", "customers_weighted").sort(
    ["run_label", "group"]
)
```

## Marginal vs Residual Cost Composition (HP vs Non-HP)

```{python}
_residual_cols = {
    "residual_vol_weighted_avg": "Volumetric",
    "residual_peak_weighted_avg": "Peak",
    "residual_percustomer_weighted_avg": "Per-customer",
}

per_customer_long = (
    pl.concat(
        [
            pl.concat(
                [
                    cross_summary_all.select(
                        "run_label",
                        "group",
                        pl.lit(benchmark).alias("benchmark"),
                        pl.lit("Marginal (economic burden)").alias("cost_source"),
                        pl.col("Economic_burden_weighted_avg").alias("usd_per_customer"),
                    ),
                    cross_summary_all.select(
                        "run_label",
                        "group",
                        pl.lit(benchmark).alias("benchmark"),
                        pl.lit("Residual allocation").alias("cost_source"),
                        pl.col(residual_key).alias("usd_per_customer"),
                    ),
                ],
                how="vertical",
            )
            for residual_key, benchmark in _residual_cols.items()
        ],
        how="vertical",
    )
)

(
    ggplot(per_customer_long.to_pandas(), aes(x="group", y="usd_per_customer", fill="cost_source"))
    + geom_col(position="stack", width=0.65)
    + scale_fill_manual(
        values={
            "Marginal (economic burden)": "#00BFC4",
            "Residual allocation": "#F8766D",
        },
        breaks=["Marginal (economic burden)", "Residual allocation"],
    )
    + facet_grid("run_label ~ benchmark")
    + labs(
        title="Cost Composition per Customer: Marginal vs Residual ($/customer/year)",
        x="Customer group",
        y="$/customer/year",
        fill="Cost source",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(figure_size=(10, 5), legend_position="bottom")
)
```

## Build Hourly Loads and Marginal Costs with CAIRO

```{python}
# Loads are the same for both runs (same ResStock inputs); build once using active-run metadata.
building_ids = metadata["bldg_id"].cast(pl.Int64).to_list()
load_map = build_bldg_id_to_load_filepath(RESSTOCK_LOADS, building_ids)

raw_load_elec = _return_load(
    load_type="electricity",
    target_year=RUN_CONFIG[ACTIVE_RUN_LABEL]["target_year"],
    load_filepath_key=load_map,
    force_tz="EST",
)

hourly_by_group = build_hourly_group_loads(raw_load_elec=raw_load_elec, metadata=metadata)
hourly_by_heating_type = build_hourly_heating_type_loads(raw_load_elec=raw_load_elec, metadata=metadata)

# Build per-run hourly DataFrames: each run has its own distribution MC (from the saved CSV)
# and its own Cambium supply costs (delivery-only run uses dummy zeros; supply-adj uses real costs).
hourly_runs: dict[str, pl.DataFrame] = {}

for run_label, run_cfg in RUN_CONFIG.items():
    run_path = run_dirs[run_label]

    # Distribution: load actual CSV written by run_scenario.py into the run output dir
    dist_costs = load_dist_mc_from_run(run_path)

    # Supply (Cambium): load directly from S3 parquet via polars
    supply_costs = load_cambium_from_parquet_s3(run_cfg["cambium_costs"], run_cfg["target_year"])
    supply_costs = supply_costs.with_columns(
        (
            pl.col("Marginal Energy Costs ($/kWh)")
            + pl.col("Marginal Capacity Costs ($/kWh)")
        ).alias("Marginal Supply Costs ($/kWh)")
    )

    costs = supply_costs.join(dist_costs, on="time", how="left")

    hrly = (
        hourly_by_group
        .join(costs, on="time", how="left")
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("Marginal Distribution Costs ($/kWh)") > 0).alias("mdc_positive"),
            pl.lit(run_label).alias("run_label"),
            pl.lit(run_cfg["label_display"]).alias("label_display"),
        )
    )
    hourly_runs[run_label] = hrly

# Active-run hourly kept as `hourly` for single-run sections below
hourly = hourly_runs[ACTIVE_RUN_LABEL]

for run_label, hrly in hourly_runs.items():
    stats = hrly.select(
        pl.col("mdc_positive").sum().alias("hours_positive"),
        (pl.col("hp_load_kwh").sum() / pl.col("total_load_kwh").sum()).alias("hp_annual_share"),
        (
            pl.when(pl.col("mdc_positive"))
            .then(pl.col("hp_load_kwh"))
            .otherwise(0.0)
            .sum()
            / pl.when(pl.col("mdc_positive"))
            .then(pl.col("total_load_kwh"))
            .otherwise(0.0)
            .sum()
        ).alias("hp_peakdist_share"),
    ).row(0, named=True)
    print(
        f"[{run_label}] mdc > 0 hours: {int(stats['hours_positive'])}, "
        f"HP annual share: {float(stats['hp_annual_share']):.2%}, "
        f"HP peak-dist share: {float(stats['hp_peakdist_share']):.2%}"
    )
```

## Stacked Aggregated Load Curves by Heating Type

```{python}
(
    ggplot(hourly_by_heating_type.to_pandas(), aes(x="time", y="load_kwh", fill="heating_type"))
    + geom_area(position="stack", alpha=0.85)
    + labs(
        title="Stacked Weighted Aggregated Load Curves by Heating Type",
        x="Time",
        y="Load (kWh)",
        fill="Heating type",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(figure_size=(12, 4), legend_position="bottom")
)
```

## Hourly Load Line Plot by Heating Type

```{python}
(
    ggplot(hourly_by_heating_type.to_pandas(), aes(x="time", y="load_kwh", color="heating_type"))
    + geom_line(size=0.55, alpha=0.9)
    + labs(
        title="Hourly Weighted Load Curves by Heating Type",
        x="Time",
        y="Load (kWh)",
        color="Heating type",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(figure_size=(12, 4), legend_position="bottom")
)
```

## Marginal Costs: Distribution vs Supply

```{python}
# Marginal costs are run-independent (same network model, same Cambium parquet);
# plot once using the active run.
cost_plot = hourly[
    ["time", "Marginal Distribution Costs ($/kWh)", "Marginal Supply Costs ($/kWh)"]
]
cost_plot = pl.concat(
    [
        cost_plot.select(
            "time",
            pl.lit("Marginal Distribution Costs ($/kWh)").alias("cost_type"),
            pl.col("Marginal Distribution Costs ($/kWh)").alias("cost"),
        ),
        cost_plot.select(
            "time",
            pl.lit("Marginal Supply Costs ($/kWh)").alias("cost_type"),
            pl.col("Marginal Supply Costs ($/kWh)").alias("cost"),
        ),
    ],
    how="vertical",
)

(
    ggplot(cost_plot.to_pandas(), aes(x="time", y="cost", color="cost_type"))
    + geom_line(size=0.5)
    + labs(
        title="Marginal Cost Time Series",
        x="Time",
        y="Marginal cost ($/kWh)",
        color="Cost type",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(figure_size=(12, 4), legend_position="bottom")
)
```

## Cross-Subsidy Components by Group

```{python}
(
    ggplot(
        cross_components_all.to_pandas(),
        aes(x="component_label", y="component_transfer_total_musd_per_year", fill="group"),
    )
    + geom_col(position=position_dodge(width=0.8), width=0.7)
    + facet_wrap("~run_label")
    + coord_flip()
    + labs(
        title="Cross-Subsidy BAT Components by Run (Total Transfer, $M/year)",
        x="Benchmark component",
        y="Transfer ($ million per year)",
        fill="Group",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(figure_size=(9, 4), legend_position="bottom")
)
```

## BAT Results by Heating Type

```{python}
ht_summary
```

```{python}
ht_bat_long = pl.concat(
    [
        ht_summary_all.select(
            "group",
            "run_label",
            pl.lit(bat_key).alias("bat_key"),
            pl.lit(bat_label).alias("bat_label"),
            pl.col(bat_key).alias("bat_usd_per_customer_year"),
        )
        for bat_key, bat_label in {
            "BAT_vol_weighted_avg": "Volumetric",
            "BAT_peak_weighted_avg": "Peak",
            "BAT_percustomer_weighted_avg": "Per-customer",
        }.items()
    ],
    how="vertical",
)

(
    ggplot(
        ht_bat_long.to_pandas(),
        aes(x="group", y="bat_usd_per_customer_year", fill="bat_label"),
    )
    + geom_col(position=position_dodge(width=0.8), width=0.7)
    + facet_wrap("~run_label", ncol=1)
    + coord_flip()
    + labs(
        title="BAT by Heating Type and Run ($/customer-year)",
        x="Heating type",
        y="BAT ($/customer-year)",
        fill="Benchmark",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(figure_size=(10, 6), legend_position="bottom")
)
```

## Consumption During Hours Where `marginal_distribution_cost > 0`

```{python}
customer_count_map = dict(zip(cross_summary["group"].to_list(), cross_summary["customers_weighted"].to_list()))
positive_hours_summary = summarize_positive_distribution_hours(hourly, customer_count_map)
positive_hours_summary
```

```{python}
(
    ggplot(
        positive_hours_summary.to_pandas(),
        aes(x="group", y="positive_dist_cost_hours_load_mwh_per_customer", fill="group"),
    )
    + geom_col(width=0.6)
    + labs(
        title="Per-Customer Load in Hours with Positive Marginal Distribution Cost",
        x="Customer group",
        y="MWh/customer-year in mdc > 0 hours",
        fill="Group",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(figure_size=(7, 4), legend_position="none")
)
```

## Tariff Components and Residual Allocation Drivers

```{python}
tariff_cfg = read_s3_json(f"{run_dir}/tariff_final_config.json")

active_tariff_key = RUN_CONFIG[ACTIVE_RUN_LABEL]["precalc_tariff_key"]
tariff = tariff_cfg[active_tariff_key]
fixed_monthly = float(tariff["ur_monthly_fixed_charge"])
vol_rate = float(tariff["ur_ec_tou_mat"][0][4])

tariff_components = build_tariff_components(
    hourly=hourly,
    cross_summary=cross_summary,
    fixed_monthly=fixed_monthly,
    vol_rate=vol_rate,
)

tariff_components.select(
    "group",
    "weighted_customers",
    "annual_load_kwh",
    "annual_fixed_charge_collected_usd",
    "annual_volumetric_charge_collected_usd",
)
```

```{python}
tariff_components_plot = pl.concat(
    [
        tariff_components.select(
            "group",
            pl.lit("Fixed charge").alias("component"),
            pl.col("annual_fixed_charge_collected_usd").alias("collected_usd"),
        ),
        tariff_components.select(
            "group",
            pl.lit("Volumetric charge").alias("component"),
            pl.col("annual_volumetric_charge_collected_usd").alias("collected_usd"),
        ),
    ],
    how="vertical",
)

(
    ggplot(tariff_components_plot.to_pandas(), aes(x="group", y="collected_usd", fill="component"))
    + geom_col(position="fill", width=0.65)
    + labs(
        title="Proportion of Fixed vs Volumetric Collected Charges by Group",
        x="Customer group",
        y="Share of annual collected charge",
        fill="Charge component",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(figure_size=(8, 4), legend_position="bottom")
)
```

### Interpretation Checklist

1. Compare each group's share of annual load vs share of load in `marginal_distribution_cost > 0` hours.
2. Compare tariff collection mechanics (flat customer + flat volumetric) against benchmark residual allocation policies (volumetric, peak, per-customer).
3. Both runs use the same Cambium P133 supply costs. The difference is `delivery_only_rev_req_passed`: run 1 (full rev-req) passes the total revenue requirement; run 2 (delivery-only rev-req) passes only the delivery portion.
4. Both runs use the same distribution marginal costs loaded from each run's `distribution_marginal_costs.csv` output (written by `run_scenario.py`).
5. To change `ACTIVE_RUN_LABEL` for the single-run diagnostics sections, update the variable at the top of the config cell.
